Title: Inactivation of the germacrene A synthase genes by CRISPR/Cas9 eliminates the biosynthesis of sesquiterpene lactones in Cichorium intybus L.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: germacrene A branch of sesquiterpene lactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies committed precursor for sesquiterpene lactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) to germacrene A, the first dedicated intermediate in sesquiterpene lactone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first dedicated/committed step; key control point supported by knockout phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Part of the CiGAS-short group; CiGAS-short genes were reported to be hardly expressed in leaves, with contribution in roots implied from mutant phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: CiGAS-short contribution is strongest in leaves and roots based on knockout phenotypes, despite low reported leaf expression.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to bitterness; proposed defense-related metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves and taproots, especially taproot tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse cultivation for 5 months; standard tissue culture and greenhouse conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in primary regenerants; amplicon Illumina sequencing of edits; LC-MS metabolite profiling of leaves and roots; GC-MS for related terpenes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CiGAS-short function strongly reduces or nearly eliminates sesquiterpene lactones in leaves and roots.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological phenotype under greenhouse conditions; inulin polymerization unaffected; possible altered defense under field conditions not tested; associated increase in phenolics and root squalene.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: A. thaliana U6 promoter (sgRNA, constitutive); parsley ubiquitin promoter driving SpCas9 (constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Three CiGAS-short genes are genetically linked on linkage group LG3. Mutant analyses indicate CiGAS-short genes account for most STL production. Residual STLs in quadruple mutants suggest another terpene synthase may produce small amounts of germacrene A.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon Illumina MiSeq sequencing for genotyping; LC-MS metabolite profiling; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value breeding/editing target to reduce bitter STLs and facilitate inulin extraction; linked CiGAS-short copies may ease introgression/editing deployment in chicory cultivars.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9-induced null mutations in CiGAS-short alleles caused near-complete loss of sesquiterpene lactones. 2. CiGAS-short genes, rather than CiGAS-L, provide the major contribution to STL biosynthesis in both leaves and roots. 3. Inactivation redirected metabolic flux, associating with increased root squalene and phenolic compounds.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: germacrene A branch of sesquiterpene lactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies committed precursor for sesquiterpene lactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) to germacrene A, the first dedicated intermediate in sesquiterpene lactone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first dedicated/committed step; key control point supported by knockout phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Part of the CiGAS-short group; CiGAS-short genes were reported to be hardly expressed in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally important in leaves and roots based on combined knockout lines.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to bitterness; proposed defense-related metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 multiplex knockout; amplicon sequencing; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes with other CiGAS-short paralogs to strong reduction/elimination of sesquiterpene lactones when inactivated.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No visible growth penalty under tested conditions; possible defense-related effects remain unknown.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: A. thaliana U6 promoter; parsley ubiquitin promoter
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interpreted as one of three linked CiGAS-short genes underlying the major STL-producing capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon Illumina MiSeq sequencing; LC-MS; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target in multiplex editing with other CiGAS-short genes to generate low-bitter chicory.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CiGAS-S2 is one of the CiGAS-short paralogs required for high STL accumulation. 2. Multiplex disruption with other short paralogs nearly abolishes root and leaf STLs. 3. Short-gene disruption is more consequential than CiGAS-L disruption.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: germacrene A branch of sesquiterpene lactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies committed precursor for sesquiterpene lactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) to germacrene A, the first dedicated intermediate in sesquiterpene lactone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first dedicated/committed step; key control point supported by knockout phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Part of the CiGAS-short group; CiGAS-short genes reportedly show little leaf expression.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally major contributor to STL production when assessed with multiplex-edited mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to bitterness; proposed defense-related metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 multiplex knockout; amplicon sequencing; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with CiGAS-S1 and CiGAS-S2 to drive STL biosynthesis; knockout contributes to near-elimination of STLs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological defects under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: A. thaliana U6 promoter; parsley ubiquitin promoter
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: sgRNA design had to account for SNPs in CiGAS-S3 compared with CiGAS-S1.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon Illumina MiSeq sequencing; LC-MS; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important multiplex-editing target in low-STL chicory breeding.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CiGAS-S3 is part of the short-gene set determining most STL accumulation. 2. Combined inactivation with other short paralogs strongly suppresses STLs in leaves and roots. 3. The study supports a dominant collective role of CiGAS-short genes over CiGAS-L.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: germacrene A branch of sesquiterpene lactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies committed precursor for sesquiterpene lactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) to germacrene A.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first dedicated step, but this paralog was not established as the major control determinant in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reported to be expressed in leaves and roots, generally one- to threefold higher than CiGAS-short genes; anticipated to contribute to leaf STL biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed throughout plant according to cited prior work, but mutant analysis indicates minor contribution to STL accumulation under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to bitterness; proposed defense-related metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 editing and genotype–metabolite comparison among lines with/without CiGAS-L mutations; LC-MS profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CiGAS-L mutation alone or in combination adds little beyond CiGAS-short knockout; CiGAS-L appears to play only a minor role in STL production under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No visible growth penalty under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: A. thaliana U6 promoter; parsley ubiquitin promoter
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located on linkage group LG9, unlike the three linked CiGAS-short genes on LG3. Despite expression, its exact biological role remains unclear and may be condition dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon Illumina MiSeq sequencing; LC-MS; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less critical than CiGAS-short for low-STL trait engineering, though full knockout including CiGAS-L can be used for maximal STL reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CiGAS-L was expected to contribute strongly because of its expression pattern. 2. Mutant comparisons showed CiGAS-L has only a minor effect on STL accumulation under tested greenhouse conditions. 3. This distinction is important for breeding because the major actionable targets are the linked CiGAS-short genes.
